assets.name: lecture_01_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 gives the clearest and most complete view of the board content with the lecturer moved far enough aside that the central interaction diagram and the left-hand Regge plot are least occluded. The later frame is not meaningfully sharper, and candidate 04 reintroduces more body occlusion at the right edge.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: And so, actually, when you draw this diagram,

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 02 gives the clearest unobstructed view of the main board equation. The pointing hand from candidate 01 no longer covers the central term, and the later frames do not reveal any additional mathematical content. It preserves both the large energy sum and the upper square-root expression well enough for note reconstruction.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: energy as being the Hamiltonian of a system,

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the most useful frame because it shows the expression in its most finished form: the Hamiltonian label on the left is visible, the summed correction terms are unobstructed, and Susskind is no longer pointing over the numerator. Earlier frames either hide the Hamiltonian identification or have the hand covering part of the main term.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: this Hamiltonian, or this expression for energy,

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_06.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 gives the clearest overall view of the board after Susskind has stepped aside. It preserves the discrete energy expression, the arrow to the continuum derivative term, and the lower integral form with the least occlusion. The other frames either block the lower expression while he is writing or cover terms with his hand while pointing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: This is derivative of x with respect to sigma squared.
assets.caption_hint: discrete string energy to continuum form
